differences: Enhancement is visible on weaker plant photos, but already-good shots pick up an artificial green/cyan cast.
Neutral backgrounds and bright floor/window regions are being color-shifted instead of mostly preserved.
Global enhancement remains too strong for high-quality captures, making some results look filtered rather than clearer.
suggestions: Reduce or skip grey-world color balancing on already well-balanced captures.
Lower plant/background vibrance and local-gain values for high-quality inputs.
Keep strong detail recovery for weak captures, but add a naturalness-preserving branch for sharp/well-lit photos.
verdict.reasoning: Current output clearly improves visibility on weaker inputs, but the high-quality path still over-processes color and global contrast. Another tuning pass is needed before the result looks consistently natural.

next_actions: Reduce or skip grey-world color balancing on already well-balanced captures.
Lower plant/background vibrance and local-gain values for high-quality inputs.
Keep strong detail recovery for weak captures, but add a naturalness-preserving branch for sharp/well-lit photos.
